name: Certify Expense Management API (Emburse)
what_it_does: Certify (Emburse) REST API for SMB and mid-market expense report management and travel booking. Enables AI agents to manage expense report creation and submission for travel and entertainment management, handle receipt capture and mileage tracking for expense itemization, access approval workflow and policy enforcement for expense authorization, retrieve corporate card transaction import and matching for card expense management, manage travel booking integration and itinerary data for business travel, access expense analytics and spend reporting for financial management, handle direct deposit and reimbursement workflow for employee payments, retrieve audit trail data for compliance reporting, manage department and cost center allocation for expense accounting, and integrate Certify expense data with QuickBooks, Sage, and payroll platforms.
use_cases: Automating expense submissions from T&E management agents
Capturing receipts from mobile expense agents
Processing approvals from expense workflow agents
Syncing expense data from accounting integration agents
not_for: Large enterprise without SMB/mid-market expense management fit
Invoice and AP management without T&E primary focus
Complex global multi-entity expense without enterprise-grade features
best_when: An SMB or mid-market company using Certify expense management wants AI agents to automate expense report processing, receipt capture, approval routing, and accounting sync.
avoid_when: PLATFORM RISK: Certify is owned by Emburse (Accel-KKR PE); Emburse portfolio consolidation may shift Certify customers to Chrome River or other Emburse products — verify roadmap stability for integration investment. API documentation for SMB-tier Certify is less comprehensive than enterprise Chrome River. Certify integration with ERP is batch-based (not real-time) limiting event-driven expense workflow automation.

auth.notes: Certify uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Developer portal at developer.certify.com. No native webhooks. REST API with JSON. Portland, Maine HQ (Certify). Acquired by Emburse (2019). Emburse is Accel-KKR PE-backed. Certify serves SMB and mid-market (primarily US). 10,000+ customers. Strong in construction, healthcare, and professional services. Competes with Concur (SAP), Expensify, and Abacus for SMB/mid-market T&E.

pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly per-user subscription; annual pricing available
pricing.notes: Portland, Maine. Certify founded 2008. Acquired by Emburse (2019). SMB and mid-market focus. 10,000+ customers. Competes with Expensify, Concur SMB, and Abacus for SMB expense management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; volume-based throttling

known_agent_gotchas: PLATFORM RISK: Emburse portfolio consolidation — Certify may be migrated or sunset toward Chrome River; verify product roadmap before long-term integration investment
No webhooks — Certify lacks native event webhooks; polling required for real-time expense status monitoring
Batch ERP sync — Certify to accounting (QuickBooks, Sage) sync is batch-based with configurable frequency; real-time accounting updates not available
Receipt OCR accuracy — automated receipt itemization via Certify OCR requires human review for ambiguous receipts; fully automated expense line-item extraction has error rate
Mileage calculation — Certify mileage tracking uses Google Maps; mileage calculation accuracy depends on route selection which may differ from actual travel
agent_readiness.security_notes: SMB expense management. SOC2. OAuth2/API key. US. Expense report and financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Expense management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Expense and receipt data.
